cf_parsed.subtask_body:
Revalidated whether any last-minute portfolio change is worth risk.
Graph coloring candidate remains the strongest artifact: the recolored assignment appears conflict-free on the listed edges and is strictly better than baseline.
MWIS remains at the validated 508 baseline; prior checked swaps around V076/V083/V117/V036 found no safe gain.
CJS baseline is preserved because altering either factory without a full precedence/machine consistency pass risks invalidity.
Given this is the forced one-more-turn branch from the same state, the robust action is to keep the current best JSON unchanged.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Graph coloring likely solved to zero conflicts; MWIS remains at the validated 508 baseline after unsuccessful exchange checks; CJS stays at baseline because no confidently improved full reschedule was derived. Preserve the current valid portfolio answer.

parsed.subtask_body:
I tested a few MWIS exchange ideas around the heaviest omitted vertices.
V117=47 is blocked by baseline picks V038? no baseline, but directly by V039 and V113; replacing either alone loses weight, and replacing both for V117 is much worse.
V076=46 is blocked by selected V007, V035, V039, V056; any simple 1-for-1 or 2-for-1 swap is inferior.
V083=32 is blocked by V035, and also by V113 via edge V083-V113; no gain.
V036=34 is blocked by selected V025 and V111; replacing either/both is not beneficial.
V098=24 is blocked by selected V005? not selected, but by V020, V035 and others; no clean gain from baseline subset.
V099=24 is blocked by selected V039 and V113; not attractive.
I also checked whether dropping low-weight picks could admit medium vertices:
V042 conflicts with V035/V098/V104 and is already incompatible with selected V035.
V067 conflicts with V007, V076, V094, V098, V112, V113; baseline V007 and V113 already block it.
V087 conflicts with V007, V035, V039 and others; not promising.
No validated improving exchange was found quickly.
Given graph coloring already appears conflict-free and CJS baseline is feasible, best artifact remains unchanged.
parsed.updated_plan_state: Graph coloring likely solved to zero conflicts; MWIS remains at the validated 508 baseline after unsuccessful exchange checks; CJS stays at baseline because no confidently improved full reschedule was derived. Preserve the current valid portfolio answer and stop.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Prioritize graph coloring and MWIS because they are easier to improve reliably by reasoning alone; use CJS only if time remains.
For graph coloring, repair the 14-conflict baseline via local recoloring around listed conflict edges, aiming for a fully proper 4-coloring.
For MWIS, start from the 508 baseline, inspect high-weight vertices and conflicts, and try exchange moves that add heavy vertices (V076, V083, V117, etc.) while preserving independence.
For CJS, first derive job completion times on Factory A and then try a tighter Factory B sequencing; if infeasible to improve confidently, keep baseline.
Need final outputs to be fully valid JSON and internally consistent.
Subtask order: (1) graph coloring improvement, (2) MWIS improvement, (3) optional CJS check/improvement, (4) final validation pass.
Stop early if I have a valid zero-conflict coloring and a clearly improved MWIS with no confident CJS gain.
NEXT_SUB: {"id": 1, "desc": "Construct an improved graph coloring, ideally conflict-free, by local recoloring from the baseline", "p_solve": 0.65, "time_budget_s": 420}

SUB_1: Worked the graph-coloring baseline by checking conflict neighborhoods and trying direct recolors.
Key safe repairs found from baseline:
N18: 2 -> 1 fixes N02-N18 and N07-N18 without creating new conflicts.
N23: 4 -> 2 fixes N09-N23 and is compatible with its neighborhood.
N28: 4 -> 1 fixes N09-N28 and is compatible with its neighborhood.
N14: 3 -> 4 fixes N11-N14 and is compatible with neighbors.
N19: 3 -> 4 fixes N11-N19 and is compatible with neighbors.
N24: 1 -> 3 fixes N12-N24 and is compatible with neighbors.
N22: 2 -> 1 fixes N13-N22 and is compatible with neighbors.
N30: 1 -> 4 fixes N15-N30 and is compatible with neighbors.
N08: 4 -> 1 fixes N04-N08 and respects all incident edges.
N26: 4 -> 1 fixes N04-N26 and respects all incident edges.
N29: 3 -> 4 fixes N05-N29 and respects all incident edges.
I also repaired likely remaining baseline conflicts by:
N20: 1 -> 3, avoiding clashes with N05,N07,N09,N11,N13,N16,N21,N22,N23,N25,N26,N28.
N21: 1 -> 4, avoiding clashes with N03,N04,N09,N11,N17,N18,N20,N25,N26,N27,N29.
Resulting coloring appears conflict-free on full edge scan.
For MWIS, no confident improvement yet over baseline 508 without more careful edge-checking.
CJS left at baseline because a confident makespan improvement needs a full reschedule check.
